score_evidence: The final phase_04c_reference_board render compares the chromatin baseline and generated scene side by side inside Blender.
The approved pass uses many thinner, sinuous magenta, violet, and limited cyan fibers instead of the rejected thick neon tubing from earlier iterations.
The chromatin occupies more of the nucleoplasm and remains inside the nuclear envelope without passing through pore gates.
Dense bead texture communicates DNA-protein packaging, while condensed chromosomes and nucleolus structures remain absent.
minor_issues: Some individual fiber ends remain visible in close-up and should be masked or looped in a later polish phase.
The chromatin still reads slightly cleaner than the baseline's rough, protein-coated rope texture.
The mesh density is too high for direct Roblox export without LOD or baking.
required_fixes: Before Roblox export, generate simplified chromatin LODs and consider baking fine bead texture into a normal map.
In the next nucleus polish pass, reduce visible strand ends and add localized roughness to the largest visible fibers.
